sections.paragraph:
ἄπιος1

a pear-tree, Lat. pirus, Arist.

ἄπιος2
Etym. The first sense has short α, the latter ᾱ.
Etym. ἀπό

I. far away, far off, distant, ἐξ ἀπίης γαίης Hom.

II. Ἄπιος, η, ον, Apian, i. e. Peloponnesian (said to be derived from Ἆπις, a king of Argos), Ἀπία γῆ, Ἀπία χθών, or Ἀπία alone, the Peloponnese, esp. Argolis, Aesch., Soph.; so Ἀπίς, ίδος, ἡ, Theocr.
